title: ranunculus aquatilis var. fluviatilis
intro: Ranunculus aquatilis var. fluviatilis, also known as the creeping buttercup or water crowfoot, is a perennial aquatic plant that thrives in shallow water environments. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Ranunculus aquatilis var. fluviatilis include its potential to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as coughs and bronchitis, as well as its ability to act as a diuretic and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often used in traditional medicine to support digestive and urinary health.
